etymology.description: phonetic 𡳿 -> 之; 士 + semantic 又 (hand) -> 寸 in Small Seal Script -> 寺
etymology.interpretation: 寺(sì, 'temple') combines the semantic element 寸 (cùn, 'inch'), originally from 又 (yòu, 'hand'), to imply precision or holding, with the phonetic element that evolved into 士 (shì, 'scholar'). The phonetic component originally represented going or movement but later took on a more abstract form. This combination suggests a place of order and ritual, characteristic of temples.

examples.chinese: 他每天早晨去寺庙(他每天早晨去寺廟)。
examples.english: He goes to the temple every morning.

examples.chinese: 寺庙里的钟声(寺廟裡的鐘聲)非常悠扬(非常悠揚)。
examples.pinyin: Sìmiào lǐ de zhōngshēng fēicháng yōuyáng.
examples.english: The bell sounds in the temple are very melodious.

examples.chinese: 我们参观了京都的古老寺庙(我們參觀了京都的古老寺廟)，觉得很平静(覺得很平靜)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen cānguānle Jīngdū de gǔlǎo sìmiào, juéde hěn píngjìng.
examples.english: We visited an ancient temple in Kyoto and felt very peaceful.
